message:
fix(codegen,types): Use class name for module-level class instance variables

Two key fixes for class instance type handling:

1. generator.zig: When pre-declaring module-level variables that are
   class instances, use the class name directly (e.g., `Rat`) instead
   of `*runtime.PyObject`. This fixes type mismatches when assigning
   Class.init() results to module-level variables.

2. expressions.zig: When both operands of a BinOp are the SAME class
   type, infer the result as that class type. This handles common
   patterns like `Rat + Rat -> Rat` instead of falling back to unknown.

Together these fixes ensure variables like `a = Rat(2, 3)` and
`result = a + b` get the correct `Rat` type instead of `*runtime.PyObject`.
